steps.title: Recognize that the conclusion targets Hemingway's oeuvre specifically
steps.content: Blatt's conclusion is about a negative correlation within Hemingway's body of work. To support it, we need evidence comparing Hemingway's own acclaimed and less-acclaimed novels to show the adverb pattern.

steps.title: Match the comparison pattern
steps.content:
D demonstrates the negative correlation: the acclaimed The Sun Also Rises contains 63 -ly adverbs per 10,000 words, while the less-acclaimed True at First Light contains 102.

A and B involve other authors, and C doesn't clearly show the pattern within Hemingway's works.
That is the correct answer.
